title: BAI Invites Expression of Interest for Professional Badminton League
indian_angle: This indicates the Badminton Association of India's efforts to develop a professional league within the country.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued an Expression of Interest (EOI) for the Professional Badminton League, signaling potential new developments for domestic badminton.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: The article details the Indian squad for the upcoming Asian Games, highlighting the return of key players like Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
summary: India's Thomas Cup winning squad is reassembling for the Asian Games, with Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand making their return, bolstering the team's chances at the prestigious continental event.

title: Indian Badminton Team Finalized for 20th Asian Games 2026
indian_angle: This official announcement confirms the Indian badminton contingent for the highly anticipated 20th Asian Games.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the final list of players who will represent India at the 20th Asian Games in 2026, following a rigorous selection process.

title: Tanvi Sharma's Game: More Saina Nehwal Than PV Sindhu?
indian_angle: This feature analyzes the playing style of young Indian talent Tanvi Sharma and compares it to two of India's greatest shuttlers, Saina Nehwal and PV Sindhu.
summary: A deep dive into the evolving game of rising Indian badminton star Tanvi Sharma, exploring whether her style aligns more with the aggressive play of Saina Nehwal or the defensive prowess of PV Sindhu.

title: Grassroots Coaches Development Program Kicks Off in Guwahati & Raipur
indian_angle: This circular announces a crucial coaches development program in Indian cities, aimed at strengthening badminton at the grassroots level.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has initiated a Coaches Development Program for grassroots level coaches in National Centres of Excellence (NCE) in Guwahati and Raipur, running from June 12th to 21st.

title: Ayush Shetty Stuns World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Ayush Shetty secured a significant victory over a higher-ranked Chinese opponent, showcasing his growing potential.
summary: Indian prodigy Ayush Shetty delivered a standout performance, defeating China's World No. 15 Weng Hong Yang, marking a notable upset in the tournament.
